title: cyathea minima
intro: Cyathea minima, also known as the dwarf tree fern, is a species of tree fern native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its small size and distinctive fronds. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its fronds which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits associated with Cyathea minima include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Cyathea minima are often made by infusing the fronds in water or using them in teas, which are said to provide relief from colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
